title: Process Safety Engineer (Operations I Process Engineering)
description:
• Well Established Company
  • Basic $4000 - $4800 + AWS + Variable Bonus
  • Excellent Welfare and Benefits + Career progression
  • Working location : Tuas (Transport Provided)
  • Working days : Monday to Friday
  • Working hours : 8am to 5pm

Job Responsibilities:

  • Develop technical studies towards the implementation of processes in the plant, evaluating plant capacity, process conditions, and other variables which can affect the process.
  • Champion a culture of Process Safety excellence at the company based on the PSM 14 elements.
  • Develops the Process Engineering Package (P&ID, PFD specification of process equipment, specification of electrical process equipment, material balances, energy balances, and DCS control logics) for the plant processes.
  • Reviewing Risk Assessment Screenings and Involve in Management of change procedure and be a team player.
  • Involve in design safety review procedure for all processes and be a team player.
  • Manage and conduct the PHA - HAZOP program, as well as track, assist and follow up with the resulting recommendations.
  • Assist with data collection for Quantitative Assessment (QRA) tools.
  • Organizing and delivering effective and efficient Process Safety studies (PSI, MOC, Mechanical integrity, Operating procedures & etc. for all plant processes as needed for stewardship of processes.
  • Develop Pre Start-up Safety Review (PSSR) for plant processes and follow up with the design review.
  • Perform risk analysis and coach others on the use of risk analysis techniques.
  • Collaborate with the teams to discover and implement long-term process improvement efforts.
  • Perform audit of process safety management systems and execute risk mitigation corrective actions.
  • Assisting Incident Investigation teams in investigating and classifying process safety incidents, as well as providing support in the development of process tools and action plans to close identified Process Safety gaps.
  • Utilizes process simulation software to test and find the most appropriate PSM strategies.
  • Other ad-hoc duties as assigned

Requirements:

  • Minimum of Bachelor’s degree in Chemical Engineering and/or Equivalent.
  • Minimum of 2 – 5 years of engineering experience supporting Operations, Engineering and/or Projects, such as plant or process engineering, project safety design, execution, engineering and/or technical service roles.

Candidates are encouraged to apply this position via Apply Now button with the following information in the resume

  • Work experiences and job responsibilities
  • Current and Expected salary
  • Reason for leaving
  • Date of availability
  • Education background

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.


THIEN CHING MEI (R2198032)

EA Recruitment Pte Ltd

EA License No: 21C0492
